title: QUIC Interop Endpoint-Host Shell Verification
scope: Verify the endpoint-host shell, the connected-UDP bridge into the library-owned runtime endpoint, the outbound datagram handoff, and the honesty of route-miss handling.
preconditions: The solution builds successfully with the shell and test-support files included.
The INT requirement-home tests can be discovered and executed locally.
Loopback UDP sockets are available in the test environment.
verification_method: Focused requirement-home tests plus build execution and artifact inspection of the shell README and gap ledger.
procedure: Run the positive and negative endpoint-host requirement-home tests to confirm routed ingress, outbound datagram emission, and unroutable route misses.
Build the solution and confirm the harness shell participates in the normal build graph without moving protocol ownership out of the library.
Inspect the README and gap ledger to confirm unsupported harness testcases remain honest and the new shell does not claim completed handshake support.
expected_result: A real connected UDP socket boundary can now drive one library-owned runtime connection, the runtime can surface an outbound handshake datagram back to the socket layer, and route misses stay unroutable. Unsupported harness testcase dispatch remains `127`.
status_summary: This verification slice proves the shell boundary only. Full handshake testcase enablement still depends on runner-side wiring and the remaining Initial/DCID bootstrap work.

supplemental_sections.heading: Deferred Coverage
supplemental_sections.content: Actual interop-runner handshake enablement, 0-RTT, 1-RTT data-path ownership, and key update are intentionally deferred. This artifact only proves the shell and its honest failure mode.
